title: Implement component codes: eBCH, dRM, and CRC for GRAND decoding
description:
Implement the short component codes needed as building blocks for product codes decoded by SOGRAND.

## Extended BCH Codes (eBCH)

Standard BCH codes extended by one overall parity-check bit, making all codewords even-weight. The existing BCH infrastructure in `gf2-coding/src/bch/` can be leveraged.

| Code | Construction | t |
|---|---|---|
| eBCH(16,11) | BCH(15,11) + 1 parity bit | 1 |
| eBCH(16,7) | BCH(15,7) + 1 parity bit | 2 |
| eBCH(32,26) | BCH(31,26) + 1 parity bit | 1 |
| eBCH(64,57) | BCH(63,57) + 1 parity bit | 1 |

Each needs: generator matrix G, parity-check matrix H, encoder, and the `is_even = true` flag for GRAND optimization.

## Decreasing Reed-Muller Code dRM(32,21)

Code family from Camion-Poli (2021). Research the construction and produce the generator matrix. GRAND handles decoding; only G and H are needed.

## CRC as Linear Block Code CRC(25,15)

CRC with polynomial `0x2b9` used as a (25,15) linear block code for error correction. Construct the systematic generator matrix from the CRC polynomial. Verify polynomial against the paper.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All codes produce correct (n,k) generator and parity-check matrices.
- `H * G^T = 0` for all codes.
- eBCH codes are confirmed even (all codewords have even weight).
- Encoding works correctly (syndrome of any codeword = 0).
- Each code's minimum distance matches the theoretical value.
- All codes expose the interface required by ORBGRAND/SOGRAND: H matrix, n, k, `is_even` flag.

## Location

Extended BCH in `crates/gf2-coding/src/bch/` (extend existing). dRM and CRC as new modules.
